name: MasteryAI
introduction:
RMIT Capstone Project (School of Software and Engineering) | March 2026 – Present

A premium, enterprise-grade AI-powered education ecosystem that supports trainers, educators, and students. The platform consists of a highly optimized React 19 / Next.js frontend and backend microservices orchestrating secure Google OAuth/JWT auth, classroom provisioning, Bring-Your-Own-Key (BYOK) LLM credentials vaults, pgvector-based RAG engines, and real-time SSE token streaming.
activities: Architected a scalable microservices infrastructure to handle high-concurrency traffic with an expected throughput of ~1,000 RPS for a target of 300,000+ users.
Optimized high-volume data handling and ingestion by leveraging message queues to decouple heavy workloads, ensuring system scalability and fault tolerance under peak loads.
Secured sensitive user data at rest by utilizing HashiCorp Vault to encrypt both API keys and chat conversations, ensuring strict compliance with Decree 13/2023 data privacy regulations.
Mitigated database bottlenecks and minimized runtime latency by utilizing an in-memory cache to store both hot database queries and transient decrypted credentials under strict TTL constraints.
problemSolved: Building a scalable AI education system requires balancing high-throughput streaming and RAG workloads with strict user access control and secure API key handling. MasteryAI resolves this by implementing a polyglot microservice layout: Java Spring Boot manages the rigid transactional structures (auth, rosters, keys), Python FastAPI/LangChain accelerates vector queries and LLM coordination, and Next.js delivers a responsive, role-adaptive web experience.
